indications.name: Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer Syndrome
indications.custom_value: 52-year-old female with newly diagnosed Stage IIB invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast (ER+/PR+/HER2-). Strong paternal-lineage family history of ovarian and breast cancer. Meets NCCN criteria for germline panel testing (Genetic/Familial High-Risk Assessment: Breast, Ovarian, and Pancreatic, v2.2025).

clinical_info.prior_testing_details: 1) Somatic tumor NGS panel (FoundationOne CDx equivalent, CPT 81455) completed 2025-09-09: PIK3CA p.E545K (pathogenic, VAF 38% — FDA-approved Alpelisib indicated), TP53 p.R175H (pathogenic, VAF 45%), CDH1 p.E243* (likely pathogenic, VAF 22% — germline correlation recommended). No somatic BRCA1/2 variants detected. TMB 8 mut/Mb (intermediate), MSI stable. 2) BRCA1 full sequence + del/dup (CPT 81162) — APPROVED by Aetna (Auth# AET-PA-20250904), performed at Invitae. 3) Pathology: core needle biopsy left breast (2025-08-28) — IDC, Nottingham grade 3 (8/9), 2.4cm, ER+ Allred 8/8, PR+ Allred 6/8, HER2 IHC 1+ negative, Ki-67 42%, LVI present, PD-L1 CPS 5.
clinical_info.supplemental_notes:
Ordering physician: Dr. Priya Venkataraman, MD, Medical Oncology, NPI 1467830295, Newton-Wellesley Cancer Center. Physician attests: 'Germline hereditary cancer panel testing (CPT 81432) is medically necessary to: (1) inform surgical planning — bilateral mastectomy vs. lumpectomy decision is contingent on germline result; (2) assess eligibility for PARP inhibitor therapy (olaparib, niraparib) should disease progress to metastatic stage; (3) guide oncologic surveillance and risk-reduction interventions per NCCN; (4) enable cascade testing for first-degree relatives.' Signed electronically 2025-09-05.

Per NCCN Guidelines (v2.2025): patient meets testing criteria — personal history of breast cancer diagnosed at age 52 (female), plus qualifying family history (first-degree relative equivalent with ovarian cancer under age 50 and breast cancer under age 50 on same lineage).

Pre-test genetic counseling provided 2025-09-04 by Emily Torres, MS, CGC (Board Cert. #2019-CGC-04517), 45-minute session. Covered: possible result categories, test limitations, hereditary implications for relatives, cascade testing options, GINA protections. Patient verbalized understanding and signed informed consent (on file).

Additional context from somatic panel: CDH1 somatic variant (likely pathogenic) raises concern for hereditary diffuse gastric cancer syndrome if confirmed germline — requires separate risk management per NCCN.
clinical_info.family_history: Paternal grandmother: ovarian cancer, diagnosed age 49, deceased age 54. Paternal aunt: breast cancer, diagnosed age 42, currently in remission. No prior genetic testing documented in family. Patient has not undergone prior germline testing. Two-generation paternal lineage pattern consistent with hereditary breast and ovarian cancer (HBOC) syndrome — meets NCCN criteria. Mother (age 80): healthy, no cancer history. Father (age 81): prostate cancer diagnosed age 75 (low-grade, watchful waiting). One brother (age 49): healthy. One sister (age 47): healthy, offered cascade testing.
clinical_info.additional_info: Prior auth status: CPT 81432 (hereditary panel) — PENDING. CPT 81162 (BRCA1 full analysis) — APPROVED (Auth# AET-PA-20250904). CPT 81433 (hereditary panel dup/del) — PENDING. CPT 81455 (somatic NGS) — APPROVED (Auth# AET-PA-20250830). Lab: Invitae Corporation, San Francisco CA. Accession: INV-2025-GRM-18840. Sample: blood EDTA, received at lab, in analysis. Expected TAT: 10-14 business days. Results to be reviewed in multidisciplinary tumor board.
